title: orobanche alba f. major
intro: Orobanche alba f. major, also known as the white broomrape, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Orobanche alba f. major include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds and skin conditions, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried extract or tincture, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the affected area.
